title: Maillage talents: Building the Community Web
video_url: Highly specific search: 'Community talent mapping and skills exchange in decentralized organizations'
sources: README.md: Role definitions and mission overview [cite: 2]
quests.ts: Foundational quest specs for District B
database.sql: PostgreSQL schema for roles and users
forom-config.ts: Default category and action labels [cite: 3]
App.tsx: Pixel economy and quest reward implementation
resume: The quest "Maillage talents" (B1) serves as the social glue for the "Équipe" district, located at the intersection of Category B and the "Échange" action (1) on the 10x10 FRM grid[cite: 3]. Within the Phase 1: The Foundations (V1) context, the goal of this quest is to facilitate the discovery and mapping of skills among community members[cite: 2]. This process is essential for achieving the broader mission of "Saving human communities and long-term memory" by ensuring that the right talents are assigned to the right pillars[cite: 2]. Technically, the results of these talent exchanges are archived as "memos" in the local PostgreSQL database, specifically within the "memos" table which links user IDs to their respective contributions. The application's modern tech stack—utilizing React 19 and Vite 7—allows for a highly performant and responsive 5x5 spatial viewport where users can navigate the grid to see who holds which expertise[cite: 2]. The "Social" role, aligned with the color blue, plays a primary role in this quest, as their alignment focuses on networking, connection, and managing community morale[cite: 2]. By hosting this talent map on local hardware like an NVIDIA Jetson, the community maintains absolute data sovereignty, protecting sensitive member information from centralized cloud platforms[cite: 2]. Economically, successfully completing the B1 quest rewards the participant with 2 Pixels (PX), aiding in the redistribution of purchasing power within the community[cite: 2]. This economy starts with the 9 Super-Mods, who each possess 500 PX to kickstart the server's growth. As the community progresses toward Phase 3, this archived "Maillage talents" data will be processed by ROM, the local RAG engine, to help the AI understand the human resources available to the node[cite: 2]. This ensures that knowledge transfer, especially in pilot projects like the 70 ETS Student Clubs, is not just about files but about the people who manage them[cite: 2]. The publication of this talent network at coordinate B1 secures the social infrastructure of the forom, providing a searchable directory of expertise that prevents "Deep Lore" from being lost when members graduate or move on[cite: 2]. Ultimately, B1 transforms individual skills into a collective, permanent asset. This documentation is vital for long-term community survival.
summary: This quest facilitates the discovery and mapping of member skills at coordinate B1, strengthening the community's social fabric and ensuring talent is effectively deployed for the mission.
